helpful: Provisioned petition/BIO/QP text — the petition appendix even included the full Second Circuit opinion, so no document retrieval was needed
Per-fee-class grant rates in statpack.json (paid vs IFP) — a much better anchor than the blended rate
Snapshot's ProceedingsandOrder list — extensions, amici, distribution date all legible
gaps: fedcourts query by --citation returned nothing for a heavily-cited SCOTUS precedent (447 U.S. 557); a topic/issue-area filter or full-text QP search over resolved cert petitions would retrieve genuinely similar priors
The prompt's 'Segment base rate by salience band' table is not present in the committed statpack, so the described salience-band anchor was unavailable
No cut in the statpack for cert-stage amicus count or interlocutory posture — the two strongest case-specific signals here had to be weighed without corpus numbers
notes: Corpus query cost ~110MB of ranged reads for zero rows; base rates came entirely from the committed statpack.
